description: To restore to original strength or condition; renewal that creates fresh vitality. 'Sunoe' names the restoring—when what was depleted becomes whole again.
sound_symbolism: The word restores with care. 'Su' sustains with renewing force, vitality returning. 'No' settles with grounded restoration, wholeness reforming. Then 'e' opens to fresh life.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb: 'mi sunoe' (I regenerate). Restores 'sunai' (health) and 'kenua' (energy). Related to 'welo' (prosper) as process to outcome.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Regenerative systems—practices that restore rather than deplete ecosystems.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Natural regeneration—the cycles of fallow and renewal that sustain fertility.
